name: Checker
comment:
Implements a checker that ensures a data model is valid as a level 2
construct.


# Model checking.

@docs check, ModelCheckingError, errorToString

unions.name: ModelCheckingError
unions.comment: The error cases the model checker can detect.

values.name: check
values.comment: Runs checks on an L1 model and lowers it to L2 if all the checks pass.

values.name: errorToString
values.comment: Converts model checking errors to strings.

name: L1

unions.name: Container
unions.comment: Containers for 0..n instances of some type.

unions.name: Declarable
unions.comment: Things that can be declared as named constructs.
cases: List.Nonempty.Nonempty ( String.String, List.List ( String.String, L1.Type pos ref, L1.Properties ) )

unions.name: PropSpec
unions.comment: Defines the kinds of additional property that can be placed in the model.

unions.name: Property
unions.comment:
Allows additional properties from a variety of possible kinds to be placed
in the model.

unions.name: Restricted
unions.comment: Restricted forms that are subsets of the basic data types.
cases: { min : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, max : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, width : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int }
{ minLength : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, maxLength : Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int, regex : Maybe.Maybe String.String }

unions.name: Type
cases: List.Nonempty.Nonempty ( String.String, L1.Type pos ref, L1.Properties )

unions.name: Unchecked
unions.comment: Indicates that the model has not been checked.

aliases.name: L1
aliases.comment: L1 is a list of unchecked declarables.

aliases.name: PropSpecs
aliases.comment:
A set of additional property kinds that can or must be defined against
the model.

aliases.name: Properties
aliases.comment: A set of additional properties on the model.

values.name: defineProperties
values.comment:
Defines a set of property specifications with possible defaults. The first argument is a
list of property specs, and the second is a list of default values.

Values may appear in the second argument that are not in the first, in which case a specification
for them will be infered.

Values in the second argument that are also in the first should be of the same kind, but will be
overriden by the second argument if not.
values.type: List.List ( String.String, L1.PropSpec ) -> List.List ( String.String, L1.Property ) -> ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties )

values.name: emptyProperties
values.comment: Creates an empty set of properties.

values.name: positionOfDeclarable
values.comment: Gets the position context from a Declarable.

values.name: positionOfType
values.comment: Gets the position context from a Type.

values.name: propertiesOfDeclarable
values.comment: Gets the properties from a Declarable.

name: L2
comment:
Defines the level 2 language for data models that have been checked for
consitency, and have been processed for general data modelling concerns without
regard to specific code generation.


# The L2 data modelling language.

@docs L2


# Ref checking status - L2 is checked.

@docs RefChecked

unions.name: RefChecked
unions.comment:
Indicates that named type in the model have been reference checked to
enusure that they name something that actually exists.

Some summary information of what they refer to is also cached during ref
checking.

name: L3

unions.name: PropCheckError
unions.comment:
Once properties have been checked, then reading properties as per the property
specification should always succeed, since those properties have been verified to be
present and of the correct kind.

If reading a property fails, it is a coding error and should be reported as a bug.
This error type enumerates the possible properties bugs.

aliases.name: DefaultProperties
aliases.comment: Allows the default properties on parts of the model to be defined.
aliases.type: { top : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), alias : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), sum : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), enum : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), restricted : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), fields : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), unit : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), basic : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), named : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), product : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), emptyProduct : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), container : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ), function : ( L1.PropSpecs, L1.Properties ) }

aliases.name: L3
aliases.type: { properties : L1.Properties, declarations : Dict.Dict String.String (L1.Declarable pos L2.RefChecked) }

aliases.name: Processor
aliases.comment: API for an L3 model processor.
aliases.type: { name : String.String, defaults : L3.DefaultProperties, check : L3.L3 pos -> ResultME.ResultME err (L3.L3 pos), errorToString : (pos -> String.String) -> pos -> err -> String.String }

aliases.name: PropertiesAPI
aliases.comment: An API for supplying property readers for various parts of the data model.
aliases.type: { top : L3.PropertyGet, declarable : L1.Declarable pos L2.RefChecked -> L3.PropertyGet, field : L1.Properties -> L3.PropertyGet, type_ : L1.Type pos L2.RefChecked -> L3.PropertyGet }

aliases.name: PropertyGet
aliases.comment: An API for reading properties of various expected kinds.
aliases.type: { getStringProperty : String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError String.String, getEnumProperty : Enum.Enum String.String -> String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError String.String, getQNameProperty : String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError (List.List String.String), getBoolProperty : String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError Basics.Bool, getOptionalStringProperty : String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError (Maybe.Maybe String.String), getOptionalEnumProperty : Enum.Enum String.String -> String.String -> ResultME.ResultME L3.PropCheckError (Maybe.Maybe String.String) }

values.name: makePropertiesAPI
values.comment:
Creates a properties API from a set of defaulted property specs, and an
L3 model.

values.name: propCheckErrorToString
values.comment: Convert prop check errors to strings.

name: Naming

values.name: checkName
values.comment:
Checks that a name starts with an alpha character, and is followed only
by alpha and numeric characters.

values.name: safeCCL
values.comment:
Checks if a name matches an Elm keyword, and proposes a different name to
use instead, which is the original with an underscore appended.

    cleanupName "type" == "type_"

The name is put into camel case starting with a lower case letter.

values.name: safeCCU
values.comment:
Checks if a name matches an Elm keyword, and proposes a different name to
use instead, which is the original with an underscore appended.

    cleanupName "type" == "type_"

The name is put into camel case starting with an upper case letter.

values.name: safeName
values.comment:
Checks if a name matches an Elm keyword, and proposes a different name to
use instead, which is the original with an underscore appended.

    cleanupName "type" == "type_"

values.name: sortNamed
values.comment: Sorts a list of named things.
values.type: List.List ( String.String, a ) -> List.List ( String.String, a )

values.name: sortNonemptyNamed
values.comment: Sorts an non-empty list of named things.
values.type: List.Nonempty.Nonempty ( String.String, a, b ) -> List.Nonempty.Nonempty ( String.String, a, b )

name: Templates.Elm

values.name: codec
values.comment: Generates a Codec for an L1 type declaration.
values.type: String.String -> L1.Declarable pos L2.RefChecked -> ( Elm.CodeGen.Declaration, Elm.CodeGen.Linkage )

values.name: lowerFun
values.comment: Lowers an L1 function type into an Elm type annotation
values.type: L1.Type pos L2.RefChecked -> L1.Type pos L2.RefChecked -> ( Elm.CodeGen.TypeAnnotation, Elm.CodeGen.Linkage )

values.name: lowerType
values.comment: Lowers an L1 type into an Elm type annotation.

values.name: typeDecl
values.comment:
Turns an L1 type declaration into Elm code.

A type can result in a list of declarations - enums in addition to declaring a
type can also declare the permitted enum values.
values.type: String.String -> Elm.CodeGen.Comment Elm.CodeGen.DocComment -> L1.Declarable pos L2.RefChecked -> ( List.List Elm.CodeGen.Declaration, Elm.CodeGen.Linkage )
